articles.title: Crude oil extends lower and stalls the fall at a trend line target
articles.expand: Crude oil moved sharply lower today on hopes for a diplomatic solution to the conflict in Iran — or at least progress toward a framework for peace through a Memorandum of Agreement. The selloff pushed the price down to a low of $90.32, where buyers stepped in near an upward-sloping trend line connecting the late-February low to the April low. Holdi...

articles.title: Gold climbs 1% as hopes for US-Iran deal pressure Oil and the US Dollar
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) rebounds sharply on Monday as hopes for a US-Iran deal to end the war in the Middle East and reopen the Strait of Hormuz weigh on the US Dollar (USD) and Oil prices. At the time of writing, XAU/USD is trading around $4,572, up 1.40% on the day.

articles.title: GBP/JPY Price Forecast: Bulls test 214.50 resistance as upside momentum improves
articles.expand: GBP/JPY trades with a positive bias on Monday as the British Pound (GBP) outperforms the Japanese Yen (JPY) amid improving market sentiment surrounding a potential US-Iran deal. At the time of writing, the cross is trading around 214.52, up 0.30% on the day.

articles.title: USDCHF bouncing off swing level support. 100 day MA remains a key target at 0.7839
articles.expand: The USDCHF extended to the downside today after finally breaking below the key 100-day moving average at 0.78393, a level that had helped hold support on Friday. Once below, downside momentum accelerated and pushed the pair down toward the May 14 swing low at 0.7807, where buyers stepped back in and stalled the fall. The bounce from that support le...

articles.title: Canadian Dollar: Losses against US Dollar look limited – Scotiabank
articles.expand: Scotiabank’s Global FX Strategy team, including Shaun Osborne and Eric Theoret, notes that the Canadian Dollar is flat against the Dollar and underperforming other G10 currencies due to its differentiated risk profile.

articles.title: Pound Sterling Price News and Forecast: GBP/USD holds onto gains near 1.3500 amid risk-on mood
articles.expand: The British Pound (GBP) clings to opening gains around 1.3500 against the US Dollar (USD) during the late European trading session on Monday. The GBP/USD pair trades higher as the market sentiment remains risk-on due to expectations that the United States (US) and Iran will reach a deal soon.

articles.title: Japanese Yen struggles for traction despite weaker US Dollar amid elevated Energy costs
articles.expand: USD/JPY consolidates on Monday after opening the week with a bearish gap as the US Dollar (USD) comes under pressure amid growing optimism that the United States (US) and Iran are moving closer toward a deal that could eventually reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: The USD is lower as markets react to the peace hopes.
articles.expand: The USD is lower as the US wakes to a holiday and they honor all those who have died on the battlefields, and the UK barbecues get heated up in observance of the May Bank Holiday. articles.title: EUR/USD Weekly Forecast: Awaiting more war-related headlines and central banks
articles.expand: The EUR/USD pair fell for a second consecutive week, settling not far above a fresh multi-week low of 1.1576. War-related headlines kept driving financial markets, coupled with mounting speculation that the United States (US) Federal Reserve (Fed) will deliver a rate hike before the year is over.
